notes.author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU) / Dr. Daniel Okafor (Medicine)
notes.preview: Transfer out of the ICU. Summarizes the course: variceal bleed → TIPS → prolonged fevers/shock with a fully negative infectious workup → comfort-measures decision → unexpected recovery after antibiotics were stopped. Final impression: presumed drug fever and drug-associated neurotoxicity. Reflects on the diagnostic anchoring.

notes.author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU)
notes.preview: Several days after antibiotics (and other non-comfort meds) were stopped, her fevers resolved, she weaned off pressors, and she woke up. With infection excluded and the timeline pointing to the drugs, the team now attributes the syndrome to DRUG FEVER and drug-associated neurotoxicity — a diagnosis not fully considered earlier.

notes.type: MICU Note — Transition to Comfort Measures
notes.author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU) / Dr. Emily Rubin (Palliative Care)
notes.department: Medical Intensive Care Unit / Palliative Care
notes.preview: After the family meeting, the surrogate (sister) and reconciling children elect comfort measures. Non-comfort interventions — including the antibiotics and vasopressors — are discontinued as part of the comfort-focused plan.

notes.author: Dr. Emily Rubin (Palliative Care)
notes.preview: Family meeting at ~2 weeks in the ICU: not improving, MELD ~40, refractory shock, unresponsive. The sister (surrogate) and the two recently-reconciled adult children raise comfort measures. Documents the family dynamics and the factors being weighed. (Deliberation; no decision enacted yet.)

notes.author: Dr. Wendy Stead (Infectious Diseases)
notes.preview: Consulted for persistent fevers. Reviews the negative workup to date and recommends continuing broad-spectrum coverage and pursuing further occult-source evaluation. The differential as documented remains focused on infection.

notes.author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU)
notes.preview: Day ~10. Persistent high fevers despite vancomycin + meropenem; now on two pressors. Broad workup resulting: CT C/A/P no abscess, repeat paracentesis negative, LP normal, blood cultures NGTD. Plan: continue/broaden antibiotics and keep looking for an occult infectious source.

notes.author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU)
notes.preview: Post-TIPS. New high fevers and hypotension requiring norepinephrine; somnolent and not waking despite weaning sedation. Presumed sepsis: blood cultures drawn, antibiotics broadened to vancomycin + meropenem. Infectious workup initiated.

notes.author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU) / Dr. Priya Menon (GI) / Dr. Sameer Iqbal (IR)
notes.preview: Massive hematemesis → MTP, intubation, ICU. Bedside EGD: bleeding varices, banding failed → emergent TIPS with hemostasis. Started ceftriaxone (variceal-bleed/SBP prophylaxis), octreotide, pantoprazole. Hemodynamically stabilized initially post-procedure.

notes.author: Dr. Olivia Reyes (Emergency Medicine)
notes.preview: 55F with alcohol-related cirrhosis and 3 months of ~20 lb involuntary weight loss, presenting with abdominal distension and dyspnea. New ascites (paracentesis negative for SBP) and bilateral effusions. Vitals: T 36.7, BP 100/60, HR 95, RR 18. Shortly after arrival: massive hematemesis.

notes.author: Dr. Rachel Imani (Hepatology)
notes.preview: Outpatient evaluation of the ~3-month unintentional weight loss in cirrhosis. Differential and initial workup documented (labs, mildly elevated AFP, plan for dedicated HCC-surveillance imaging). Ongoing alcohol use; counseled.

notes.author: Dr. Steven Lowe (PCP)
notes.preview: Early report of fatigue and decreased appetite with some weight loss in a patient with known cirrhosis and ongoing alcohol use. Alcohol-cessation counseling; hepatology follow-up arranged.

_status: 11 notes (2 historical + 9 course across 3 timepoints). The notes deliberately embody the team's INFECTION ANCHORING through TP2 (broad-spectrum escalation, ID consult focused on occult source) and NEVER name drug fever until the gated TP3 recovery note. The CMO deliberation (NOTE006, TP2) does not enact cessation; the antibiotic-stop + recovery + drug-fever recognition are gated to TP3 (NOTE007-009) so Q3/Q4 are answered without the outcome.
